rulings.Case Title: DENNIS T. MANGANO VS. THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Dennis Mangano'S Motion To Set Hearing Re Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 22, 2013, Line 17: Plaintiff Dennis Mangano's Motion To Set Hearing Re Petition For Writ Of Mandate The motion is denied as unnecessary. Petitioner does not need a court order to calendar his petition for writ of mandate. Petitioner may file and serve its motion for writ of mandate as a regular law and motion matter. The Court hears such matters Monday through Friday at 9:30 a.m. All parties are to lodge courtesy copies of their filings in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The filings should comply with CCP 1005(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JOHN DOE I et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Quash Subpoena [Ccp 2029.600, 1987.1, 1987.2)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 22, 2013, Line 1, PETITIONER JOHN DOE's Hearing On Petition To Quash Subpoena [CCP 2029.600, 1987.1, 1987.2]. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Petitioners JOHN DOE 1 and JOHN DOE 2's Petition to Quash Subpoena is GRANTED. Respondent has failed to establish a prima facie case for the causes of action stated in the Complaint dated August 13, 2013. Prior to issuing its Order, the Court will hear argument concerning categories 3 and 4 of Exhibit A of the subpoena at issue, dated September 5 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL COLLEGIATE STUDENT LOAN TRUST 2007-3, A VS. CHRISTOPHER NORBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 22, 2013, Line 9: DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER NORBERG'S Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint Granted. Defendant shall file his proposed Cross-Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Pursuant to CCP § 426.50, the Court shall liberally permit compulsory cross-complaints to be filed, as long as the defendant acted in good faith. There is no evidence that Defendant did not act in good faith. Further, Plaintiff has not established actual prejudice. The trial date is set for June 2014, which provides sufficient time for discovery pertaining to the two causes of action in the proposed cross-complaint to be conducted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRE BARBASHOV et al VS. CINDERELLA BAKERY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 22, 2013, Line 12: DEFENDANT CINDERELLA BAKERY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied as it does not comply with CRC 3.1113(l). Defendant's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Plaintiff Yevgenia Lipkina. Plaintiffs are given 10 days leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts that Yevgenia is a successor in interest to decedent Rimma Lipkina. Defendant's demurrer to the first and fourth causes of action on the grounds Plaintiffs failed to allege sufficient facts to state a claim is overruled. The Court cannot determine, as a matter of law, the substance that allegedly caused decedent's death was natural to the preparation of the food. (See Mexicali Rose v. Superior Court (1992) 1 Cal.4th 617, 630.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FLYNN AND ENSLOW, INC. VS. DARREN WELTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Darren Welter'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 22, 2013, Line 18: Defendant Darren Welter's Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint Defendant's motion to strike is denied. Defendant fails to substantively address the merits of his motion to strike in the moving Memorandum of Points and Authorities. Instead, Defendant addresses the merits of the motion, for the first time, in his Reply brief. (See Balboa Ins. Co. v. Aguirre (1983) 149 Cal.App.3d 1002, 1010.) Furthermore, Defendant improperly includes legal argument in his notice of motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FLYNN AND ENSLOW, INC. VS. DARREN WELTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Darren Welter'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 22, 2013, Line 18: Defendant Darren Welter's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint ("AC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted as to Exhibits A and B under Evid. Code § 452(d). Judicial Notice is granted to Exhibits C-G only to the extent that the documents exist and the parties involved. The content of the documents is are not capable of immediate and accurate determination under Evid. Code § 452(h). The Court vacates its Order of July 17, 2013, as the Register of Actions shows that Plaintiff filed the AC on July 16, 2013 as a matter of right under CCP § 472. Defendant's demurrer to each cause of action on statue of limitations grounds is overruled. Plaintiff alleges facts that it did not discover Defendant's alleged wrongful conduct until Defendant met with Kim Fox-Yoder and an outside accountant on or around July 2012 and Defendant returned his work laptop. (AC 23-25.) Defendant's demurrer on the grounds that Plaintiff' failed to allege sufficient facts to state a claim is sustained with leave to amend as to the first, second, and third causes of action, and overruled as to the Fourth, Fifth and Sixth causes of action. As to the first and second causes of action, Plaintiff fails to allege sufficient facts that Plaintiff had economic relationships with third parties that were disrupted. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to plead facts, if it can so in good faith, regarding the third party or parties that Defendant misdirected business from and the business relationships Defendant disrupted. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff fails to plead facts with the requisite specificity required for a fraud claim. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to plead facts regarding Defendant's intent to induce Plaintiff's reliance and Plaintiff's substantial reliance. Defendant's demurrer to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action are overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim. Defendant's demurrer to each cause of action on the grounds of uncertainty is overruled as the facts allow Defendant to reasonably determine what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts or claims are directed against him. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG
